expectations.text: Runs 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py create-range <file> Inp_Discount_Rate Assumptions B8'
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py create-range ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Discount_Rate Assumptions B8'. Result: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Discount_Rate", "reference": "Assumptions!B8"}.

expectations.text: Runs 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py write <file> Inp_Discount_Rate 0.08' to set the value
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py write ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Discount_Rate 0.08'. Result: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Discount_Rate", "value": 0.08}.

expectations.text: Verifies the range was created correctly (either via read or inspect)
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py read ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Discount_Rate'. Result: {"status": "ok", "values": {"Inp_Discount_Rate": 0.08}}.

expectations.text: Uses the correct IDFA naming convention (Inp_ prefix for an input assumption)
expectations.evidence: Named Range created as 'Inp_Discount_Rate' — uses Inp_ prefix correctly for an input assumption.

expectations.text: Does not manually edit the xlsx with openpyxl — uses the idfa_ops.py script
expectations.evidence: All operations use idfa_ops.py: create-range, write, read. No direct openpyxl usage mentioned.
